name: LUXE 2 Brunswick St Terrace Apartment Pet Friendly
description: LUXE 2 is a gorgeous 2 bedroom terrace apartment, that is centrally located between 3 of Brisbane's favourite hotspots Fortitude Valley, James Street and New Farm. This apartment is one of two boutique apartments, located on the top floor of The Tsiknaris Building. The space is freshly refurbished & boasts an outdoor terrace with a great view for entertaining. A perfect executive residence,  a business trip or an ideal location for a small active family, wanting to discover Brisbane at its best!
neighborhood_overview:
New Farm has cafes on every corner. It's home to a world-renowned performance and function centre (The Powerhouse). Leafy streets lined with charmingly renovated workers’ cottages. A single bend of the Brisbane River away from the CBD. Plus a picturesque 37-hectare park to boot. Better still it's only a stones throw from Brisbane's latest hotspot, Howard Smith Wharves.

At a walking distance of two kilometres from the CBD, New Farm was surprisingly named for its beginnings as a farming settlement. It is one of Brisbane’s oldest and most iconic areas, and consequently one of the city’s most desirable postcodes. One of those rare spots that somehow manages to exude an inner-city hipster vibe while simultaneously maintaining its feel as a tight-knit community.

neighborhood_overview:
Stay at the heart of Brisbane's vibrant cityscape! This modern location on Adelaide Street offers guests an ideal blend of urban energy and scenic beauty, with the Brisbane River, cultural highlights, and a lively dining and shopping scene all just moments away. Perfect for both relaxation and adventure, your stay here puts you within walking distance of Brisbane’s most cherished attractions and hidden gems, ensuring a memorable city experience.

Key Areas Within Walking Distance:

✔︎ Queen Street Mall - Brisbane’s premier shopping destination, with high-end boutiques, dining, and entertainment options.

✔︎ Eagle Street Pier - Stunning riverside dining precinct offering renowned restaurants and picturesque river views.

✔︎ Howard Smith Wharves - A bustling waterfront hub with trendy bars, eateries, and beautiful views of the Story Bridge.

✔︎ Brisbane Riverwalk - Enjoy a scenic walk along the river with easy access to Kangaroo Point and

neighborhood_overview:
Experience the perfect blend of tranquility and convenience in our peaceful neighborhood. Just a short 7-minute walk (500m) will take you to Arcoona Street near Valhalla Street, where you can catch bus 123 at stop 74, providing direct routes to Garden City and Sunnybank Plaza. For those who prefer the train, Altandi Station is just a 20-minute walk (1.4 km) away, offering easy access to the Brisbane CBD, the Gold Coast, and the airport.

If you're a fan of vibrant food scenes and nightlife, you'll love that Market Square, a 16-minute (1.2 km) walk away, is known for its delicious Asian cuisine and lively atmosphere. This neighborhood offers a peaceful retreat with everything you need within easy reach.

neighborhood_overview: Carina, located just 7km east of Brisbane's CBD, is a charming suburb that offers a relaxed, family-friendly atmosphere with convenient access to the city's highlights. Its main draw is its proximity to Carindale Shopping Centre, one of Brisbane's largest retail and entertainment hubs, featuring a wide array of shops, dining options, and a cinema complex. Within a 5-kilometer radius, visitors can also explore scenic parklands such as Whites Hill Reserve, perfect for bushwalking and picnics, and enjoy easy access to vibrant cafes and restaurants in neighboring suburbs like Camp Hill and Coorparoo. With excellent transport links, Carina is a great base for travelers seeking a peaceful retreat while staying close to Brisbane's bustling attractions.

neighborhood_overview:
Toowong is a lively riverside suburb blending natural beauty, rich culture, and urban convenience, making it ideal for travelers seeking a vibrant yet relaxing stay. Just minutes from the Brisbane River, this area offers a mix of green spaces, charming cafes, retail therapy, and an easy commute to Brisbane’s iconic attractions. Whether you’re visiting for work, study, or leisure, Toowong delivers the best of Brisbane’s lifestyle and access to the city’s highlights.

Key Attractions within Walking Distance:

✔︎ Toowong Village Shopping Centre - A shopping haven with diverse retail stores, cafes, and a large supermarket for essentials.

✔︎ The Regatta Hotel - An iconic riverfront pub known for its Queenslander architecture, with great dining, drinks, and live entertainment.

✔︎ Brisbane River and Bicentennial Bikeway - Perfect for scenic walks, jogs, or cycling along the river, with views of the city skyline.

✔︎ University of Queensland

name: Central Brisbane Escape | Pool, Gym & Free WiFi
description: My apartment is positioned relatively well in Newstead. With Fortitude Valley and Brisbane City just a short drive away, and all the dining, retail, education, employment and entertainment these bustling districts provide. Newstead offers incredible contemporary living, combining atmosphere with eye-catching architecture and rich cultural experience. My apartment is designed to help you feel at home when you travel, we bring home to you.
neighborhood_overview:
Ideally positioned, just 5 minutes* from the CBD via public transport – in the heart of the city’s most in vogue postcode. Numerous haunts of the hip and well-heeled are all only a stroll from your door. They include the new 17ha Newstead Riverpark, the Gasworks - known for its chic cafes and restaurants - the distinctive art nouveau facade of the Waterloo Hotel, James Street’s epicurean tree-lined boulevard and The Emporium, with its delicious array of cafes, bars, and restaurants.

10 minute walk from Fortitude Valley, the city’s cultural melting pot, home to Chinatown, and one of Brisbane’s main focal points for eclectic nightlife, fine dining, cafes, bars and quirky boutiques.

With easy access to main arterial roads and regular bus, ferry and CityCat services on your doorstep, being well connected to the greater Brisbane region is a breeze.

- Boundary Street West End is the place to be for all things entertainment, with bars and plenty of live venues nearby.

- Have an excellent breakfast at either of the coffee shops right outside the building (I have heard the $5 brekkie sandwich right next door is amazing)

- Visit Brisbane City Hall and Museum of Brisbane, and hang out in King George Square (there's usually something going on )
- Visit the Wednesday street markets at Brisbane Square for lunch and experience amazing international cuisine (outside Treasury Casino)
- Head across Kurilpa Bridge (the pedestrian bridge outside the building) to the cultural and theatre district. There you can wander through GOMA, Qld Art Gallery, Qld museum and State library at no cost.

I love all things outdoors, being amongst nature and experiencing new cafes & fresh organic food.

My life motto Is:- Be the change you wish to see in the world.
Kalie

neighborhood_overview: Brisbane City, or Brisbane CBD, is the beating heart of Queensland's capital. Nestled along the meandering Brisbane River, this vibrant suburb boasts a skyline adorned by modern skyscrapers juxtaposed against historic sites like the City Hall and the charming Old Windmill. It's a bustling hub where commerce, culture, and leisure converge — home to numerous restaurants, shops, and entertainment venues like the South Bank Parklands, which offer a kaleidoscope of experiences, from riverside picnics to art exhibitions. Its accessibility, with an extensive network of public transportation, and its lively atmosphere make Brisbane City a magnet for locals and tourists seeking an eclectic blend of urban allure and riverside tranquillity.

name: Your own garden cottage, handy to everything
description:
You'll love the leafy views from our self-contained garden cottage.

We are high on the hill in convenient Mitchelton with great NNE aspect. It's 150m to a fabulous suburban cafe and not far to a major shopping centre, the suburbs quaint shopping area and train - 18 minutes to town.

The studio space has a well-equipped kitchenette, bathroom, washing machine, TV, unlimited WiFi and air-con. There's a made queen size bed (and if needed a spare mattress with linen supplied for guests to make)
neighborhood_overview:
Mitchelton is one of Brisbane's older suburbs, with plenty of young families now arriving. Traditional post war weatherboard housing and terracotta tiled roofs dot the many hills in our suburb, along with some newer development.

What was our corner store 150 metres away is now an up-beat cafe called Mitch and Antler.

Our suburb's traditional nearby centre - Blackwood Street - offers a variety of cafes and restaurants, bakeries, a convenience store, newsagency, gift shops, medical centre and a post office.

Blackwood Street hosts the Jan Powers Farmers Markets on the first Sunday of every month.

The regional shopping centre, Brookside, is also within walking distance, or there is a regular bus right past our door.

neighborhood_overview: Love my neighbourhood and my neighbours as they were very welcoming when I first moved into my place. They keep a watch on me and my place and bring in my garbage bins every week which I really appreciate. I love the fact that I live so close to the big venues such as the Entertainment Centre used for concerts, shows and the really big events. Close by Chermside Shopping Centre which is a huge shopping centre that not only you can shop till you drop but it has bowling, cafes, restaurants, pub and the best part the movies all 10 min walk from house. I am surrounded by two large parks, Marchant Park and Lorikeet Park which are nice for a stroll and a five minute walk to get to and both have bicycle tracks. There is the RSL, Aqauatic Centre and Library all side by side and only 10min walk or 5min drive which is very handy indeed. My locations is in a prime location as it has five shopping centre  with restaurants, coffee shops and pubs surrounding it. Not bad I say and love it.
host_about:
I am friendly, kind, generous, warm hearted and enjoy good conversations about travel, family almost anything really. I am full time healthcare worker treating children and sometimes adults as well but I am not a nurse. I have travelled extensively throughout the world and Australia with New York and London being my favourite cities in the world and Turkey my most favourite country to visit. In saying that though I still call Brisbane home. I enjoy cooking up a storm in the kitchen, fluffing around in my yard and garden and going to week-end markets.   I live a stones throw from the public pool and numerous gyms. 
I like a glass of wine on a week-end and to kick back and chill out. 
I enjoy watching the cricket and the occasional day at the horse racing where I can we some and lose some. 
I am now into trying to make my garden and yard a tranquil space for my guests to enjoy even more.

name: Brisbane Warehouse Apartment CBD 2Bd 2Bath parking
description:
An Architect designed 2 x bed, 2 x bathroom unique warehouse apartment called Studebaker on 2 levels, in central Brisbane.

Stroll to the CBD, funky Fortitude Valley, Central Train Station, Roma St or simply across the road to the Supermarket & local cafes. Airconditioning in all rooms / WIFI / Family + Work Space Friendly.

We are in the same area as the Sofitel Brisbane CBD offering quality accommodation (incl your own kitchen facilities, + secure parking) for a fraction of the price.
neighborhood_overview:
We are a beautiful converted Warehouse Apartment containing only 6 x boutique 2 level town house style apartments. We are located in Spring Hill one of the oldest residential neighbourhoods in Brisbane, with many houses dating back to the nineteenth century. This building was originally the Brisbane Brake and Steering Warehouse (you will see this written on the original facade above the garage entrance). You will love the original brick work contrasted with the modern apartment. Also the high lofted ceilings, exposed beams and original signage found throughout the building.

This apartment was architecturally designed and has been in our family for over 12 years. It is our home away from home. We want you to cherish the space as much as we do.

You will be staying at UNIT 4. (No other apartments are available THIS IS A PRIVATE RESIDENCE).
Our apartment features 2 bedrooms, 2 bathrooms, 2 balconies a large downstairs living area and kitchen - airconditioned in
host_about:
We look forward to having you as our guests.
I lived in this unique apartment for 6 + years so know its excellent features and how convenient it is to all that Brisbane has to offer. I am also well traveled to appreciate good, clean & safe accommodation.

description: This high-rise, bright 1-bed located in the vibrant Fortitude Valley is the perfect spot for you to immerse in Brisbane's relaxed and fun atmosphere. Offering spacious interiors and only a 4-min drive to the Brisbane Riverwalk and attractions, this home will give you easy access to restaurants, shopping, transport, an indoor pool and a fully-equipped gym. neighborhood_overview: Fortitude Valley, commonly known as "The Valley," is a vibrant inner-city suburb of Brisbane. Steeped in history, it was named after the ship "Fortitude" that transported convicts in the 1820s. Today, it stands as a cultural hub with a thriving arts, music, and nightlife scene, hosting a plethora of live music venues, art galleries, and theatres. The area is renowned for its diverse shopping experiences, ranging from boutique stores to high-end fashion boutiques, with popular precincts like the Emporium and James Street. Fortitude Valley also offers a wide array of dining options, showcasing global cuisines and trendy restaurants. Connected by a railway station and several bus routes, it remains an accessible locale. With an energetic nightlife, the area hosts events and festivals. Fortitude Valley is an ever-evolving, cosmopolitan destination that caters to diverse interests and lifestyles.

host_about:
It is my absolute pleasure to welcome you to SuperBnBHosts and our array of beautiful bespoke & boutique apartments across South East Queensland.

Long and Short Term Accommodation options available throughout Brisbane and Sunshine Coast. All price points!! Feel free to reach out at 
@ : Admin AT Superbnbhosts com

SuperBnBHosts are locally based in Brisbane and it has been our purpose and passion to provide the best accommodation and experience in Queensland.

I have personally picked each apartment, styled and furnished for the ultimate guest experience and stay. My focus is to make sure each apartment has a warm, cosy and homely feel while still keeping it classy with that LUXE feel. 
I want my guests to experience a comfortable and relaxed vibe, instead of that sterile hotel feel.

My team and I have hand picked and trained a small group of amazing cleaners within Brisbane to ensure every apartment gets the attention to detail it deserves and ultimately my guests get a sparkling clean space. This also comes with a cleanliness guarantee - if my guest isn't happy, my cleaner will be back in within 24 hours.

My passion is all about a living a holistic and healthy lifestyle and I LOVE sharing this by using Healthy, Natural, Eco Friendly & Organic products within the apartments for my guests to use and experience. They will find healthy organic shampoo, body wash, soaps and the list goes on.

My team know that my No. 1 priority are my guests. We will always do our best to support and serve the best way we can. For You!!

I love all things outdoors, being amongst nature and experiencing new cafes & fresh organic food.
I am very active, respectful and enjoy & appreciate the little things in life.

My life motto Is:- Be the change you wish to see in the world.
Kalie 
Blavius :)

name: Exceptional Teneriffe Heritage Apartment
description: Combining heritage charm, luxury and sophistication, this stunning Woolstore residence has been elegantly refurbished. neighborhood_overview: Teneriffe is a bustling inner city suburb and with the adjacent suburbs of New Farm, Newstead and the Valley, there is something on offer for everyone. neighborhood_overview:
Neighbourhood overview
We wouldn't live anywhere else in Brisbane other than New Farm and we are excited to share why we love this suburb so much. Voted Brisbane's most liveable suburb many times, New Farm is on a historic inner city peninsular only 1.8 kms from the CBD. In both walking directions from 'Aotea', award winning restaurants and cafes are a mere 5 minutes away. Further afield within a 5 minute drive, New Farm also boasts the James Street Restaurant precinct, Coles and IGA supermarkets, two cinema complexes, the Powerhouse Theatre, the Judith Wright Centre of Contemporary Arts, 24 hour gyms, hairdressers, delicious takeaways, and glorious New Farm Park, which hosts a weekly Saturday Farmer's Market and features one of Brisbane's best children's playgrounds. The adjacent suburbs of Teneriffe , Newstead, and Fortitude Valley are also vibrant and full of clubs, bars, high end fashion boutiques, homeware stores and restaurants.
host_about:

Please enjoy.

neighborhood_overview:
One of Queensland’s most coveted bayside suburbs, Wynnum offers picturesque ocean views out to Moreton Island and Stradbroke Island. Soak up the sea breeze as you walk along the peaceful Esplanade, throw out a line off the Wynnum Jetty, or stop in at one of the many outstanding cafes and restaurants for a meal and enjoy the laidback lifestyle that Wynnum is known for.

A beloved seaside destination for friends and families alike, Wynnum is host to a number of kid-friendly locations, including a large tidal wading pool, Whale Park and various play areas. Just a ferry ride away from the stunning Tangalooma, Moreton Island and Stradbroke Island, Wynnum is an ideal vantage point for day trips around Queensland’s beloved Moreton Bay region.

With so much to see and do and plenty of wonderful spots to relax and spend time with the ones you love, Wynnum is the perfect seaside retreat for the whole family.

Temple Retreat is a luxurious and relaxing hideaway situated in Pullenvale, a rural acreage area west of Brisbane, 20 minutes from the Brisbane CBD and 35 minutes from Brisbane airport.

Temple Retreat is set on 10 private acres and is newly refurbished. The retreat has 4 generous bedrooms and can comfortably accommodate up to 8 guests.

There is a separate yoga studio, Seed Yoga, on the property that offers group & private yoga and meditation classes, as well as massage.
neighborhood_overview:
Pullenvale is a unique rural environment which is only 25 min drive from Brisbane CBD.

There are 3 shopping areas within a 5-10 minute drive of the retreat, Kenmore, Pullenvale Marketplace, and Bellbowrie. These offer convenience stores, restaurants, supermarkets, gyms, and service stations

description: This unique apartment, situated within walking distance of several iconic landmarks in Brisbane, such as the Gallery of Modern Art, Queensland Museum, and the State Library, offers an elegant blend of sophistication, convenience, and outstanding building amenities. With self-check-in, bright interiors, modern furnishings, and easy access to all the offerings of the CBD and its surroundings, immerse yourself in Brisbane's vibrant essence with this chic urban escape.
neighborhood_overview: South Brisbane, on the southern bank of the Brisbane River, is a dynamic inner-city suburb at the heart of Brisbane. It's famed for its cultural precinct, housing the Queensland Art Gallery, Queensland Museum, Queensland Performing Arts Centre (QPAC), and State Library of Queensland, offering a rich tapestry of exhibitions and events. The picturesque South Bank Parklands, with its gardens, walking paths, and the popular Streets Beach, provide a serene escape along the river. The suburb also boasts a diverse culinary scene, a vibrant educational presence, and easy connectivity via public transport, including the South Bank train station. With a mix of residential, commercial, and cultural spaces, South Brisbane provides a lively urban experience with splendid views of the river and city skyline, making it an appealing destination for both residents and visitors.

host_about:
Ever since I was little, I always dreamt about travelling to new places for my own little adventures. I have backpacked throughout Southeast Asia, Europe and parts of South America. I have stayed in all sorts of accommodations from luxury hotels to a couch in someone's living room. Some of my experiences were absolutely magical while some could be horrifying. 

Inspired by my own travelling experiences, I have moved back to Australia and started hosting myself at the very beginning of 2020 during covid times. Some friends started to ask me to help them with hosting while they were abroad and little by little I have started my own small business helping like-minded people to put their homes on Airbnb! 

We wish to provide all our guests unique experiences with all our beautiful homes and make their travel unforgettable!

name: Inner City Jungle, heated pool, great location
description:
Welcome to a tasteful, central and spacious apt in an ideally located part of South Brisbane. Walking distance to the city & South Bank (10mins), Suncorp Stadium (15mins), West End (5mins) & Gabba (25mins)

You will find all the amenities you need in a relaxed and tastefully decorated space; a fully equipped kitchen and laundry, large pool and secure parking, with the vibrant nightlife of Fish Lane, West End and South Bank at your doorstep.

neighborhood_overview:
I love this space. It's close to everything. E- scooters are always nearby but everything is in walking distance.

Bars: West End has a great selection and the trendy Fish Lane is only a block away.
Restaurants: So many to choose from. Chu the Phat, La Luna (both on Fish Lane), the variety of cuisines at West Village (5 min walk) or South Bank, not to mention Little Greek Taverna and Lefkas at West End.
Things to Do: Swim at South Bank, ride the ferris wheel, visit the museum or take in a show at the Cultural Centre. The well-known West End Markets operate Sat till 2 and there are night markets at South Bank every Friday.
Shops: A great variety in West End on Boundary St (5 mins). Woolworths and Harris Farm are both at the West Village and Coles is an even shorter stroll. There is a late hour IGA 2/3 blocks away on Melbourne St.
host_about: I'm a primary school teacher and recently purchased and renovated my apartment in South Brisbane. I love the location and all that the city life has to offer. My guidebook is a work in progress and I hope to update more over the next few weeks.

neighborhood_overview:
The Wynnum/Manly/Lota Foreshores extends from the mangrove edge of Elanora Park in Wynnum, south to the tidal mangrove forest at the mouth of Lota Creek. Notable features along the foreshore include Elanora Park, Oyster Point, Greene Park, Pandanus Beach, Wynnum Jetty, Wynnum Wading Pool, Darling Point, Manly Beach, Norfolk Point, Manly Boat Harbour and Fig Tree Point. A ribbon of small parks runs along most of the length of the foreshore interrupted only by the Manly Boat Harbour.

Expansive views of Moreton Bay and its islands are afforded from most locations along the foreshore. Views extend from the Port of Brisbane at Fisherman’s Island, around to St Helena Island, the sandhills of Moreton Island, to Green Island and Stradbroke Island. The Wynnum/Manly/Lota Foreshores is popular for picnics and promenading, walking, cycling, fishing, boating, informal sports and games, swimming, windsurfing and birdwatching.

host_about:
Ever since I was little, I always dreamt about travelling to new places for my own little adventures. I have backpacked throughout Southeast Asia, Europe and parts of South America. I have stayed in all sorts of accommodations from luxury hotels to a couch in someone's living room. Some of my experiences were absolutely magical while some could be horrifying. 

Inspired by my own travelling experiences, I have moved back to Australia and started hosting myself at the very beginning of 2020 during covid times. 3. Lone Pine Koala Sanctuary: A 10-minute drive away or accessible by public transport, you can meet a koala, hand-feed kangaroos, and engage with a large variety of Australian wildlife.

4. Eye on Indooroopilly Heritage Trail: This local attraction takes you on a journey through 17 points of interest that have shaped Indooroopilly’s history.

host_about:

I am a 12 year resident of Rainbow Bay, our slice of paradise, and I honestly couldn't imagine living anywhere else but right here! I am  passionate about this stunning Gold Coast location and all that it has to offer.  For me, it allows a continual experience of everything in life that I love and brings me immense joy.....amazing ocean, river & mountain scenery, spectacular sunrise and sunsets, dolphin & whale spotting, snorkelling, abundant casual and high end dining options , walking & bike tracks for as long as you can physically conquer, great shopping,  and my greatest love ....yoga studios galore :) Oh yes, did I mention it's world renowned surf breaks of Snapper Rocks and Duranbah perhaps the best in Australia!!! (ok maybe I am a bit biased :):) .......
 And of course it represents all that anyone needs for a relaxing, quiet (or not so quiet).... surf, sun and sandy holiday.

Being a friendly, social and helpful person by nature, I am excited and willing to assist you with anything you might need... be it my tips of where to dine, which beach to go to, to avoid the wind :) or simply how to heat your private hot tub ........  I am more than happy to assist with all :) 
I’m just a text message away :)

host_about:
Ever since I was little, I always dreamt about travelling to new places for my own little adventures. I have backpacked throughout Southeast Asia, Europe and parts of South America. I have stayed in all sorts of accommodations from luxury hotels to a couch in someone's living room. Some of my experiences were absolutely magical while some could be horrifying. 

Inspired by my own travelling experiences, I have moved back to Australia and started hosting myself at the very beginning of 2020 during covid times. Some friends started to ask me to help them with hosting while they were abroad and little by little I have started my own small business helping like-minded people to put their homes on Airbnb! 

We wish to provide all our guests unique experiences with all our beautiful homes and make their travel unforgettable!

host_about:
Ever since I was little, I always dreamt about travelling to new places for my own little adventures. I have backpacked throughout Southeast Asia, Europe and parts of South America. I have stayed in all sorts of accommodations from luxury hotels to a couch in someone's living room. Some of my experiences were absolutely magical while some could be horrifying. 

Inspired by my own travelling experiences, I have moved back to Australia and started hosting myself at the very beginning of 2020 during covid times. Some friends started to ask me to help them with hosting while they were abroad and little by little I have started my own small business helping like-minded people to put their homes on Airbnb! 

We wish to provide all our guests unique experiences with all our beautiful homes and make their travel unforgettable!

neighborhood_overview:
Welcome to Coorparoo, a vibrant and historic suburb nestled just 5.7 kilometers southeast of Brisbane's central business district. With a rich history dating back to its Aboriginal origins and its subsequent settlement in the mid-19th century, Coorparoo offers a unique blend of the past and the present, making it an exciting destination for travelers.

Coorparoo's name is thought to derive from an Aboriginal name for Norman Creek and carries a rich Aboriginal heritage. The Coorparoo clan lived south of the Brisbane River, and the area was known for its frequent corroborees, traditional gatherings featuring music and dance, along the banks of Norman Creek. The suburb continued to grow and flourish after white settlement, with early development spurred on by the construction of the bridge at Stanley Street and the extension of the tramway to Buranda in the late 19th century​​.

Today, Coorparoo is a bustling neighborhood that's home to a wealth of attractions and amen

Their Brisbane property blends creativity, community, and a love for the outdoors, offering a unique space for guests to enjoy.

Brisbane City

01. But first, coffee.
When it comes to good coffee, The City centre is ready to greet you with quality and specialty cafes using the latest and greatest brewing methods. Start your day with one of the best brews in Brisbane. We recommend Brew, Coffee Iconic, Coffee Anthology or Industry Beans. If you need a hearty breakky to go with your coffee, here are a few of our favourite spots.
02. Shop up a storm
The City is a fashion lover's dream destination with more than 500 stores ranging from local boutiques to international, high street and luxury brands. Start in the centre at Brisbane’s largest open air shopping precinct, the famous Queen Street Mall where hundreds of stores await. If you're looking for something specific, we’ve pulled together a guide to shopping in the heart of Brisbane City.

host_about:
Ever since I was little, I always dreamt about travelling to new places for my own little adventures. I have backpacked throughout Southeast Asia, Europe and parts of South America. I have stayed in all sorts of accommodations from luxury hotels to a couch in someone's living room. Some of my experiences were absolutely magical while some could be horrifying. 

Inspired by my own travelling experiences, I have moved back to Australia and started hosting myself at the very beginning of 2020 during covid times. Some friends started to ask me to help them with hosting while they were abroad and little by little I have started my own small business helping like-minded people to put their homes on Airbnb! 

We wish to provide all our guests unique experiences with all our beautiful homes and make their travel unforgettable!

neighborhood_overview:
Perched on the cusp of the Brisbane city centre and Spring Hill, we are within easy walking distance to the best of what Brisbane has to offer.

To the south is the bustling city, with the main shopping street, the GPO and Town Hall a 5 minute walk from our house.

To the North and East, Spring Hill is one of the oldest residential neighbourhoods in Brisbane, with many houses dating back to the nineteenth century. Along the streets, you will find quaint workers cottages and terrace houses, along with beautifully restored heritage-listed buildings. There are many fine award-winning eateries in the area, as well as cute coffee shops, and historic pubs.

To the west around the corner are the beautiful Roma Street Parklands, where you can feed the ducks , have a BBQ and take the boardwalk high above a forest canopy.

neighborhood_overview:
The historic riverside suburb of Teneriffe has been transformed from its industrial woolstore days a century ago to become of the coolest inner-city neighborhoods of Brisbane.

Its heritage-listed woolstores have been converted into trendy apartments and the streets are home to cool bars, cafes, restaurants, an antique center, parks, artisan bakery and a microbrewery while along the river is a heritage submarine walk (when submarines were stationed here during the Second World War).

Teneriffe is home to some of the best bars and restaurants that Brisbane has to offer, include our favorites, Beccofino and Hikari, all in just walking distance. You'd find charming coffee shops around and walk to James Street to enjoy its world class shopping, many bar and restaurant choices, and Palace Cinema. neighborhood_overview:
West End is arguably the historic multi-cultural heart of Brisbane. Its shops, restaurants and bars reflect its immigrant and hippy roots and provide its eclectic charm. The big weekend market at Davies park sees sellers of olives, organic foods, oranges and gourmet take aways alongside the designers of clothes, shoes, toys, coffee, home made cakes and sweets and all to the sounds of live music.

West End bars showcase aspiring musicians, sell craft beers and cocktails or provide a quiet space for people watching. There are dozens of restaurants showcasing food of the mediterranean, south east asia, africa, australia, india and a fusion of all cuisines. Coffee is big in West End - short, long, espresso or other, italian, french or fair trade. Need a frock, a suit, tie dye shirt, kimono, shoes, organic grocer, a newspaper, junk food - its all here! For a bit of down time walk along the river bank through Orleigh Park to Southbank for a swim and for more food, coffee and

name: Cosy 1-Bed with River Views, Pool, Gym & BBQ
description: Explore the vibrant atmosphere of Brisbane in this cosy 1-bed apartment with stunning river views. Featuring a snug design this modern property offers an on-site pool, gym and communal BBQ facilities. Situated in the heart of the city the property is located within walking distance of cafes, shops, restaurants, bars and the Howard Smith Wharves. With a private balcony and outdoor seating, this property is the perfect escape for couples wanting to explore the city.
neighborhood_overview: Brisbane City is the energetic heart of this iconic metropolis. Bustling with activity there's no limit to the number of things to see. Popular with shoppers peruse the busy Queen St Mall selection from luxury brands to boutique stores. For those looking to indulge themselves, you’ll never be far from a vibrant cafe, bar or restaurant that’ll meet all your culinary needs. Situated along the banks of the Brisbane river, experience the stunning waterfront walks or pop into the lush Botanic Gardens, perfect for picnics or experiencing the weekly markets. Bursting with adventure Brisbane City is the destination for your next trip.

If you need a hearty breakky to go with your coffee, here are a few of our favourite spots.
02. Shop up a storm
The City is a fashion lover's dream destination with more than 500 stores ranging from local boutiques to international, high street and luxury brands. Start in the centre at Brisbane’s largest open air shopping precinct, the famous Queen Street Mall where hundreds of stores await. If you're looking for something specific, we’ve pulled together a guide to shopping in the heart of Brisbane City.

name: Stylish unique haven in vibrant area with pool
description:
A peaceful luxury retreat in the heart of the action. A quiet, leafy, freshly built studio apartment with a stylish pool. Located in an affluent, lively and safe inner city suburb of the tropical capital Brisbane. Walking distance from cafes, restaurants, Suncorp stadium and public transport. Offers a unique tree house style loft bed to delight children and working space for business travellers.
The guest suite is attached to a quaint period home surrounded by luxurious gardens and trees.
neighborhood_overview:
Passionate antique hunter and coffee connoisseur, start with a gourmet cappuccino and croissant 100m from the house and take a stroll down Latrobe Terrace to galleries, op shops, cafes and restaurants, enjoying the stunning views.

Mad for footy or concerts, walk 20 min (1.5km) to Suncorp stadium and the famous Caxton street. This state of the art stadium seats 52,000 people and is home to the Reds, the Broncos and the Socceroos. Also check their calendar for world class music performers.

15 min walk down the hill, you will find Rosalie Village with amazing delicatessen, boutique wine bar, park and playground.

Here for business, you will be in the city in 15 min with 3 bus options (stops 200m from home) and can work quietly in the studio. Take away dinner options are on the way back from the bus stop.

Family gateway to sunny Queensland, enjoy the pool and entertainment area with darts and mini table tennis. Delight your kids with a tree house

The neighbourhood is home to cute cafes, vintage shops and one-off boutiques. Don’t miss Gramps Café, Little Red Bakery and Botanica Real Food.

name: 'Shells on the Bay'........Right on the foreshore!
description:
This private apartment like space has just been totally renovated and has a private entry with direct access to the pool and plenty of balcony space overlooking the marinas of Manly. Any closer to the waterfront and you'd be swimming.
It's fully fitted out for a longer term stay if required.
The Manly Village centre is very close but far enough away to be out of earshot. Walking to the centre is via the harbour wall, a peaceful stroll with yachts and power boats less than 50 metres away.
neighborhood_overview:
Manly is a very small community with a great village feel and situated right on the foreshore of Moreton Bay. On the weekends there are markets, Sundays see old and young sailors racing radio controlled yachts from the marina wall. You will be surprised who you can meet here.
Cyclists traverse the roads, boardwalks and cycling paths, you can ride for many kilometres in total safety.
Walkers are prolific from dawn to dusk, take a walk up the hill to the Great Wall of Manly, a 'depression work project' created to employ men after the war and funded by the Australian Government.
Travel to the many Islands within Moreton Bay by ferry or water taxi, there is so much to do you won't be bored.
And to top it all off, if you are here for a regatta or sailing event, you will not find a B&B closer to the Royal Queensland Yacht Squadron. It's a very short walk (only minutes), we know because we do it all the time.
host_about:
Shelley & I love to entertain and setting up this B&B has been exciting for us. I have a hospitality background as part of my life and Shelley is fantastic with detail and taking care of people.
We are very active, love sailing, camping, cycling, dancing and skiing.  Our knowledge of where to go and what to do is reasonably extensive as we are well travelled and have a broad knowledge of Australia.
We know Moreton Bay and it's many islands first hand, (we sail on it) and can direct guests to may interesting destinations.  Our membership of the Royal Queensland Yacht Squadron will give you an opportunity to sail on the bay as  our guest if that is something you would love to do.

description: Paradise Palms Tangalooma (Villa 35) is a newly renovated villa and is 1 of a few villas that has fully ducted air-conditioning, internet, an Oven and your own outdoor shower. It is located at Tangalooma Resort on Moreton Island. The villa is beautifully presented with new furniture and a 70-inch smart T.V for your enjoyment, with open plan living downstairs and bedrooms upstairs (sleeps 6 guests). Relax and enjoy the fantastic sunsets from your deck each evening. All this literally on the beach
neighborhood_overview:
Tangalooma, nestled on the pristine shores of Moreton Island, is a captivating island resort that offers a blend of natural wonders and adventure, making it a sought-after destination in Queensland, Australia.

Visitors to Tangalooma are greeted by breathtaking landscapes, including powdery white sand beaches, clear azure waters, and lush native vegetation. What sets Tangalooma apart is its famous dolphin feeding program. Every evening, guests gather at the resort's jetty to hand-feed wild bottlenose dolphins, an experience that fosters a deep connection with these intelligent marine mammals.

name: Bardon Retreat-luxury bed & bath
description:
Our Bardon Retreat is a newly renovated space nestled in the leafy, quiet suburb of Bardon which neighbours Red Hill, Paddington and Ashgrove. Our home is only 30 metres from the Ithaca Creek bike track which winds it's way next to the creek past the Bronco's Leagues club towards the city.

This inner city area of Brisbane boasts the quaint character Queenslander homes made of 'timber and tin' and offers some of the best boutique shopping and dining in Brisbane.
neighborhood_overview:
Bardon is a charming inner-city suburb of Brisbane, located just 4.5 kilometers from the CBD. It is known for its tree-lined streets, beautiful parks, and proximity to Mount Coot-tha, one of Brisbane's most popular tourist destinations. The beautiful Mt Coot-tha Botanical Gardens is a short drive away and neighbours the Mt Coot-tha National Park with many walking tracks to choose from. Enjoy the views, abundant wildlife and Slaughter Falls.

Our Bardon Retreat is located closer to Red Hill, Paddington and Rosalie, and in close proximity to local shops, groceries, cafes, restaurants and bars, as well as the Bronco's Leagues Club and Suncorp Stadium.

neighborhood_overview:
An upmarket suburb that encourages an outdoor lifestyle, New Farm features tree-lined streets and unique spaces such as Brisbane Powerhouse and the heart of the suburb, New Farm Park. The park attracts visitors from near and far with its perfect riverside picnic spots, cycling loops and tree house-style adventure playground.

The Riverwalk links back to the city centre via waterfront pedestrian and bike paths that meander past luxury character-filled Queensland homes.

Also ever-growing is the popularity of boutique shopping strip James Street. Design hotel The Calile Hotel resides here, alongside an array of equally suave retailers. The food-inclined will be more than satisfied, with some of Brisbane’s most favoured fine diners and coffee stops nudging artisanal food stores.

Shopping: James Street is known for its Australian fashion and homewares stores, while the laneways around Brunswick Street Mall are home to independent retailers and unique boutiques.

host_about:
Ever since I was little, I always dreamt about travelling to new places for my own little adventures. I have backpacked throughout Southeast Asia, Europe and parts of South America. I have stayed in all sorts of accommodations from luxury hotels to a couch in someone's living room. Some of my experiences were absolutely magical while some could be horrifying. 

Inspired by my own travelling experiences, I have moved back to Australia and started hosting myself at the very beginning of 2020 during covid times. Some friends started to ask me to help them with hosting while they were abroad and little by little I have started my own small business helping like-minded people to put their homes on Airbnb! 

We wish to provide all our guests unique experiences with all our beautiful homes and make their travel unforgettable!

neighborhood_overview:
We are right on the cusp of the Brisbane Airport Precinct. 7-10 minutes from the airport terminals.

My property backs on to open acreage with horses. From here you can easily go anywhere into the city, down to the Gold Coast or up to the Sunshine Coast.

Brisbane is a beautiful city with a beautiful river running through it. A city with an array of fun and exciting things happening each day & night of the week. It's all up to you.

Public transport all around Train Bus Taxi Uber & the City Cat. You can take a walk along Kingsford Smith Dr and experience the 1 billion dollar upgrade. There is Southbank. Fortitude Valley. Kangaroo Cliffs. Sunnybank. Westend. Newstead.

If you are driving it is just a turn onto the M1 which is the main highway to either coastal destinations. Or you can just turn into the tunnel (the M7) to go into the CBD.

Shopping, Gyms, Cinemas and alfresco restaurants & cafes are all around. Adjoining suburbs are

neighborhood_overview:
The place is located in Fortitude Valley, Queensland, Australia.
Fortitude Valley is a contradiction in itself – raw, yet sophisticated. It is where elegance and style meet grungy and offbeat, and heritage-listed properties proudly stand among contemporary buildings. The Valley, as it is affectionately known, was Australia’s first dedicated entertainment district and continues to be a hive of activity. Live music thrives and international DJs are drawn to the clubs and chic bars. The impressive Chinatown Mall is a hub of exotic Chinese supermarkets and restaurants. The Emporium complex channels a European village feel, James St showcases Australian fashion labels and Brunswick St is home to multicultural dining options. More recently, the Howards Wharf redevelopment at the base of the New Farm cliffs under the story bridge has recently opened. Not only do they offer a free bus shuttle service in the immediate area, a short three minute walk up Martin street will also deliver you t

Inside, enjoy a renovated master ensuite, a modern kitchen flowing into a cozy living area, and a covered patio—great for outdoor gatherings. Book now & reconnect with loved ones.
host_about:
Ever since I was little, I always dreamt about travelling to new places for my own little adventures. I have backpacked throughout Southeast Asia, Europe and parts of South America. I have stayed in all sorts of accommodations from luxury hotels to a couch in someone's living room. Some of my experiences were absolutely magical while some could be horrifying. 

Inspired by my own travelling experiences, I have moved back to Australia and started hosting myself at the very beginning of 2020 during covid times. Some friends started to ask me to help them with hosting while they were abroad and little by little I have started my own small business helping like-minded people to put their homes on Airbnb! 

We wish to provide all our guests unique experiences with all our beautiful homes and make their travel unforgettable!

neighborhood_overview: Brisbane City, or Brisbane CBD, is the beating heart of Queensland's capital. Nestled along the meandering Brisbane River, this vibrant suburb boasts a skyline adorned by modern skyscrapers juxtaposed against historic sites like the City Hall and the charming Old Windmill. It's a bustling hub where commerce, culture, and leisure converge — home to numerous restaurants, shops, and entertainment venues like the South Bank Parklands, which offers an array of experiences, from local markets to art exhibitions. Its accessibility, with an extensive network of public transportation, and its lively atmosphere make Brisbane City a magnet for locals and tourists seeking an eclectic blend of urban allure and riverside tranquility.

neighborhood_overview:
Welcome to Coorparoo, a vibrant and historic suburb nestled just 5.7 kilometers southeast of Brisbane's central business district. With a rich history dating back to its Aboriginal origins and its subsequent settlement in the mid-19th century, Coorparoo offers a unique blend of the past and the present, making it an exciting destination for travelers.

Coorparoo's name is thought to derive from an Aboriginal name for Norman Creek and carries a rich Aboriginal heritage. The Coorparoo clan lived south of the Brisbane River, and the area was known for its frequent corroborees, traditional gatherings featuring music and dance, along the banks of Norman Creek. The suburb continued to grow and flourish after white settlement, with early development spurred on by the construction of the bridge at Stanley Street and the extension of the tramway to Buranda in the late 19th century​​.

Today, Coorparoo is a bustling neighborhood that's home to a wealth of attractions and amen

Their Brisbane property blends creativity, community, and a love for the outdoors, offering a unique space for guests to enjoy.

neighborhood_overview:
Kangaroo Point is well known for the Kangaroo Point Cliffs, which are popular with rock climbers, as well as the gardens below which are popular for BBQs and picnics. The cliffs and gardens enjoy fantastic views of the Brisbane River, the City Botanical Gardens and the city skyline. Another famous Brisbane landmark in Kangaroo Point is the Story Bridge. The bridge is a vital piece of infrastructure for the city, but it also a beautiful landmark in its own right, especially when lit up in the evenings. The Story Bridge is also home to the Story Bridge Adventure Climb, which allows visitors to scale the mighty bridge and enjoy some of the best views of Brisbane. neighborhood_overview:
New Farm/Teneriffe is a riverside neighbourhood and is one of Brisbane’s most admired areas. Our suburb boasts a blossoming collection of eateries and boutiques to cater to its increasing population, not to mention the suburb is also home to the much-loved New Farm Park and cultural haven Brisbane Powerhouse. The Riverwalk links back to the city centre via waterfront pedestrian and bike paths that meander past luxury character-filled Queensland homes.

Also ever-growing is the popularity of boutique shopping strip James Street. Design hotel The Calile Hotel resides here, alongside an array of equally suave retailers. The food-inclined will be more than satisfied, with some of Brisbane’s most favoured fine diners and coffee stops nudging artisanal food stores.

description: Immerse yourself in artistic charm at our East Brisbane retreat. Adorned with unique artwork and quirky touches, this short rental is a creative haven. neighborhood_overview: East Brisbane, located on the doorstep of Brisbane's vibrant city center, offers a myriad of attractions for visitors seeking a diverse and engaging experience. Immerse yourself in the local culture by exploring the iconic Gabba Stadium, a hub for sports enthusiasts and concert-goers alike. Take a leisurely stroll through the lush Mowbray Park, offering serene riverfront views and perfect picnic spots. For art lovers, the nearby Queensland Maritime Museum showcases the maritime history of the region through captivating exhibits. Indulge in the diverse culinary scene, from trendy cafes to upscale dining along Stanley Street. Additionally, East Brisbane provides easy access to the scenic Kangaroo Point Cliffs, offering breathtaking panoramic views of the city skyline. Whether you're interested in sports, culture, nature, or gastronomy, East Brisbane promises an enriching and enjoyable visit for every type of traveler.

neighborhood_overview: South Brisbane, on the southern bank of the Brisbane River, is a dynamic inner-city suburb at the heart of Brisbane. It's famed for its cultural precinct, housing the Queensland Art Gallery, Queensland Museum, Queensland Performing Arts Centre (QPAC), and State Library of Queensland, offering a rich tapestry of exhibitions and events. The picturesque South Bank Parklands, with its gardens, walking paths, and the popular Streets Beach, provide a serene escape along the river. The suburb also boasts a diverse culinary scene, a vibrant educational presence, and easy connectivity via public transport, including the South Bank train station. With a mix of residential, commercial, and cultural spaces, South Brisbane provides a lively urban experience with splendid views of the river and city skyline, making it an appealing destination for both residents and visitors.

neighborhood_overview:
Fortitude Valley is a vibrant inner-city suburb located in Brisbane, Australia. Known for its energetic atmosphere and diverse offerings, it attracts visitors with its lively nightlife, cultural scene, shopping precincts, and entertainment venues. The suburb is home to a range of shopping precincts, including the iconic James Street. Known for its fashion boutiques, specialty stores, and high-end retailers, James Street offers a unique shopping experience. Additionally, the Valley Markets, held on weekends, provide an opportunity to explore local vendors offering fashion, arts, crafts, and fresh produce. Situated just northeast of Brisbane's central business district, Fortitude Valley is easily accessible by public transport, including train and bus services. It offers a convenient base for exploring other nearby attractions and is in close proximity to popular destinations like the CBD and South Bank.

neighborhood_overview: Fortitude Valley, commonly known as "The Valley," is a vibrant inner-city suburb of Brisbane. Steeped in history, it was named after the ship "Fortitude" that transported convicts in the 1820s. Today, it stands as a cultural hub with a thriving arts, music, and nightlife scene, hosting a plethora of live music venues, art galleries, and theatres. The area is renowned for its diverse shopping experiences, ranging from boutique stores to high-end fashion boutiques, with popular precincts like the Emporium and James Street. Fortitude Valley also offers a wide array of dining options, showcasing global cuisines and trendy restaurants. Connected by a railway station and several bus routes, it remains an accessible locale. With an energetic nightlife, the area hosts events and festivals. Fortitude Valley is an ever-evolving, cosmopolitan destination that caters to diverse interests and lifestyles.

neighborhood_overview:
Paddington is one of the most beautiful suburbs in Brisbane. It is very green and has lots of hills. There are many cafés, restaurants and boutique shops.
It's only 20 minutes walk to the city and about 8 minutes by bus, 5-6 by car.
We have views of Mt Coot-tha, a wonderful place to visit to see for kilometres. There are also some great walks in that area. The botanical gardens are at the base of the mountain as well as an observatory.
It is lovely to walk around the Paddington area but you need to be fit to ascend the hills. Bring your best walking shoes!

I love to travel and meet people from different cultures. I don't travel much any more so the world must come to me.

host_about:
Mark and I have lived in Australia for 22 years, the last 15 of those have been in Brisbane in the suburb of Highgate Hill which is very close to the city and South Bank parklands and restaurants. name: Le Petit Art Déco
description:
In a prized location surrounded by stunning Moreton Bay figs that frame the streetscape of one of Brisbane's most iconic, trendy and central suburbs, this stunning Art Deco apartment is the perfect place to stay! Beautifully presented with 1920’s decor and iconic heritage architecture, this magnificent, light-filled apartment offers an alluring combination of original aesthetic and comfort.

The apartment is perfectly located, walking distance to all New Farm and central Brisbane has to offer.
neighborhood_overview:
It’s hard not to get immediately drawn into the charm of New Farm and Teneriffe. These lively inner-city riverside suburbs are steeped in a rich history, with many of the fabulous boutiques, bars and restaurants hidden within historic restored woolstores and rustic warehouses or old Queenslander homes.

New Farm encourages an outdoor lifestyle, with tree-lined streets and unique spaces such as arts venue the Brisbane Powerhouse and the heart of the suburb, the sprawling green New Farm Park. The 37-hectare park attracts visitors from near and far with its perfect riverside picnic spots, cycling loops and tree house-style adventure playground. In late October/November the jacaranda trees that ring the huge park bloom a sea of purple flowers.

Local coffee shops and restaurants are a draw card to this fabulous area, the river walk is a must do and a sight not to be missed is the locals favorite Howard Smith Wharves.

You’ll also find Sandgate Village with some quirky boutiques, organic & fair trade shopping. Woolworths and ALDI can also supply any necessities you need.

Of a morning if you walk along the waterfront, you will see personal training groups, yoga and Pilates classes taking place in the many parks. Sandgate Parkrun is on every Saturday morning at 7am along Flinders Pde. The kite flyers are also amazing to watch.

The foreshore is a great spot to have a picnic, walk to the beautiful Shorncliffe Pier or just sit and people watch.

"Robyn's Nest" is just an easy 3 minute stroll from the local shops which include an IGA minimart, pharmacy, bottle shop, post office, bakery, cafes and restaurants. Also a very easy drive to the Boondall Entertainment Centre and Brisbane airport.

host_about:
Ever since I was little, I always dreamt about travelling to new places for my own little adventures. I have backpacked throughout Southeast Asia, Europe and parts of South America. I have stayed in all sorts of accommodations from luxury hotels to a couch in someone's living room. Some of my experiences were absolutely magical while some could be horrifying. 

Inspired by my own travelling experiences, I have moved back to Australia and started hosting myself at the very beginning of 2020 during covid times. Some friends started to ask me to help them with hosting while they were abroad and little by little I have started my own small business helping like-minded people to put their homes on Airbnb! 

We wish to provide all our guests unique experiences with all our beautiful homes and make their travel unforgettable!

host_about:
Ever since I was little, I always dreamt about travelling to new places for my own little adventures. I have backpacked throughout Southeast Asia, Europe and parts of South America. Welcome to Fortitude Valley
Fortitude Valley has always marched to the beat of its own drum. It was Australia’s first dedicated entertainment district and continues to celebrate live music, with a nightlife that sees Brunswick Street Mall’s population of bars and clubs buzzing well into the early hours.

Fortitude Valley’s James Street is a cultural staple with a top-tier fashion precinct, home to boutiques from both local and international designers and brands. Even more alluring is the stylish strip’s eateries, which harbour some of Brisbane’s best restaurants like Bianca, Hellinka and sAme sAme just to name a few.
Head in early for brunch in Fortitude Valley
King Arthur, Harvey’s and The New Black are our top picks for your daily brunching adventures but we'll let you be the judge. South Bank is Brisbane’s premier lifestyle and cultural destination. Located on the southern banks of the Brisbane River, across from the busy business and shopping centre, it is open 365 days a year and attracts an estimated 11 million visitors annually.

South Bank stretches for over two kilometers from the Queensland Art Gallery and the Gallery of Modern Art (GOMA) in the north to the Queensland Maritime Museum in the south and is famous for its diverse mix of entertainment, recreation and leisure activities that are all within walking distance of each other. Just some of the precinct’s highlights include 17 hectares of inner-city parklands; more than 90 restaurants, cafes, bars and boutiques; world-class museums and galleries; a performing arts centre; a cinema; five-star hotels and serviced apartments.

neighborhood_overview:
South Bank Park is a strip-shaped water park which located on the south bank of the Brisbane River. This is the best place to enjoy Brisbane's subtropical climate and is the perfect place for outdoor picnics. There are clear artificial beaches and lush meadows in the park. You can take a walk or barbecue here, take a cruise on the river, or watch a street show in the square and walk into the cinema to watch the movie on the big screen (Imax).

The Brisbane Ferris Wheel is an iconic building in the park, like the London Eye Ferris Wheel. The fully enclosed compartment is air conditioned and can be lifted to 60 meters for a total of 13 minutes.
The most striking of the South Bank Park is The Grand Arbor, which is very close to the Ferris wheel. The large rattan frame is like the trail and stretches for a total length of 1 km.
